summary_text: **Findings** - **High**: `main` is cleanly pushed (`HEAD == origin/main` at `4b48069ae`) but the working tree is heavily dirty (many tracked modifications + many untracked files). This creates high accidental-commit risk on future `git add .` / `git commit -a`. - **High**: Mixed operational and CI files are currently modified outside the pushed scope, including [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml), [fugue-watchdog.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-watchdog.yml), and [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md). A routine commit from this tree can unintentionally ship broad behavior changes. - **Medium**: Runtime/generated state artifacts are mixed into the same tree under [`.fugue/`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue) and [`.project-os/`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.project-os), increasing noise and making intent review harder. - **Medium**: Many untracked scripts/tests exist in production paths (for example [scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh), [tests/test-kernel-scheduler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-scheduler.sh)); accidental inclusion is likely unless isolation is enforced. **Recommendation** Safest next action is to **stop committing from this dirty `main` worktree** and switch all new work to a clean sibling worktree at the pushed commit, without touching current local changes: ```bash git worktree add ../fugue-orchestrator-clean 4b48069ae ``` Then operate only in `../fugue-orchestrator-clean` for future commits/pushes. This reduces operator risk immediately and preserves all unrelated local work exactly as-is.
